reasoning: The Lean formalization faithfully captures the informal statement. While it uses `f : ℝ → ℝ` instead of restricting to `[1,3] → ℝ`, the bound `-1 ≤ f x ≤ 1` is only imposed on `x ∈ Set.Icc 1 3` and the integrals are only over `(1,3)`, so values outside `[1,3]` don't affect the set of attainable `t` values. The use of `Set.Ioo 1 3` vs `[1,3]` for integration differs only on a measure-zero set, so integrals coincide for measurable functions. `IsGreatest` correctly encodes "how large can it be" since the classical maximum log(4/3) is actually attained (by f=1 on [1,2] and f=-1 on [2,3]). The quantifier structure (∃ f satisfying the constraints such that the integral equals t) matches the informal intent that we consider all admissible f and ask for the maximum of the resulting integral values.
